mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with improved corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.15 in 60%+ of 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and industrial production signals robust economic demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in 50%+ of cases, or their combined predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.25% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 5.25%, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns exceeds -0.3 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation above 210 compresses real interest rates and raises equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 210, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL (Rank IC) falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real sales figures indicates strong consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved retail and construction sector performance.
failure_condition: The joint growth of HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 5d SPX returns in 50%+ of cases, or their combined predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE > 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns exceeds 0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.
